indication: Atrial fibrillation on anticoagulation, hypertension, diabetes mellitus, evaluate intracranial and cervical vasculature for atherosclerotic disease and aneurysm
technique: 3D time-of-flight MRA of the circle of Willis and 2D time-of-flight MRA of the neck vessels were performed without contrast administration due to chronic kidney disease (eGFR 42).

intracranialArteries.ica: Bilateral intracranial internal carotid arteries are patent with normal caliber.
intracranialArteries.mca: Bilateral middle cerebral arteries and major branches are patent without stenosis.
intracranialArteries.aca: Bilateral anterior cerebral arteries are patent. Normal anterior communicating artery.
intracranialArteries.pca: Bilateral posterior cerebral arteries are patent. Normal caliber.
intracranialArteries.vertebrobasilar: Basilar artery is patent and of normal caliber. No stenosis.
intracranialArteries.circleOfWillis: Complete circle of Willis. No intracranial aneurysm or arteriovenous malformation.

extracranialArteries.commonCarotid: Bilateral common carotid arteries are patent.
extracranialArteries.internalCarotid: Mild signal heterogeneity at the right carotid bifurcation, consistent with mild atherosclerotic plaque. No hemodynamically significant stenosis. Left ICA is patent without significant disease.
extracranialArteries.externalCarotid: Bilateral external carotid arteries are patent.
extracranialArteries.vertebral: Bilateral vertebral arteries are patent with antegrade flow. Codominant. No stenosis or dissection.

findings.incidentalFindings: Mild periventricular white matter T2 hyperintensities on localizer images, consistent with chronic microvascular ischemic changes.

impression: No significant intracranial arterial stenosis or aneurysm.
Mild atherosclerotic changes at the right carotid bifurcation without hemodynamically significant stenosis.
Mild chronic microvascular white matter changes noted incidentally.
recommendations: Consider dedicated brain MRI if neurological symptoms develop
comparison: MRA Head and Neck dated 2019-10-07. Stable mild atherosclerotic changes at right carotid bifurcation. No interval progression.
